name: Orchestrate a routine task with HTTP / REST API and MySQL on autopilot

parameters.errorMessage: Missing required field 'type' on incoming job.

parameters.content: =✅ Orchestrate a routine task with HTTP / REST API and MySQL on autopilot — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 1 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.type }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Route by Work Type · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: standard, priority, batch + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'standard' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert · ③→ Fast-Track 'batch' + Alert · ④→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'standard' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Job Tracker', matched on `jobId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert

Posts a message to Slack (`#automation`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Fast-Track 'batch' + Alert

Posts a message to Slack (`#automation`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per job (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Selects rows from the MySQL table (`returnAll`) — the read side of the store.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Discord · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#automation`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 MySQL connection

# Orchestrate a routine task with HTTP / REST API and MySQL on autopilot

**Pattern:** Workflow Automation · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Orchestrate a routine task with HTTP / REST API and MySQL on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual process orchestration is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Required Fields Present? → Route by Work Type → Process 'standard' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Fingerprint for Idempotency → Drop Already-Processed Items → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to workflow automation without manual effort
- Anyone automating process orchestration in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; MySQL connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your process orchestration process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
